reasoning: Multiple news and analysis reports published in January and February 2026 clearly indicate that the Iranian regime has not collapsed as of early 2026. Articles such as "Is the Iranian Regime About to Collapse?" (January 2026) and "Trump: Iran regime change 'would be the best thing to ever happen'" referencing the current Iranian government (February 2026) confirm that the Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and his government remain in power in Tehran. While major protests reportedly began in late 2025 and a war occurred with Israel in June 2025, none of these resulted in the regime losing control of the central government in 2025. Therefore, the probability of regime change in 2025 is effectively zero.
